title: AWS Config Rule
description: Schema for an AWS Config rule resource, representing a compliance rule that evaluates whether AWS resources conform to desired configurations.

ConfigRuleName.description: The name that you assign to the AWS Config rule.

ConfigRuleArn.description: The ARN of the AWS Config rule.

ConfigRuleId.description: The ID of the AWS Config rule.

Description.description: The description that you provide for the AWS Config rule.

Scope.description: Defines which resources trigger an evaluation for the rule.

ComplianceResourceTypes.description: The resource types of only those AWS resources that you want to trigger an evaluation.

TagKey.description: The tag key that is applied to only those AWS resources that you want to trigger an evaluation.

TagValue.description: The tag value applied to only those AWS resources that you want to trigger an evaluation.

ComplianceResourceId.description: The ID of the only AWS resource that you want to trigger an evaluation.

Source.description: Provides the rule owner, rule identifier, and notifications that cause the function to evaluate your AWS resources.

Owner.description: Indicates whether AWS or the customer owns and manages the AWS Config rule.

SourceIdentifier.description: For AWS managed rules, a predefined identifier. For custom rules, the ARN of the Lambda function.

SourceDetails.description: Provides the source and type of the event that causes AWS Config to evaluate your resources.

InputParameters.description: A string, in JSON format, that is passed to the AWS Config rule Lambda function.

MaximumExecutionFrequency.description: The maximum frequency with which AWS Config runs evaluations for a rule.

ConfigRuleState.description: Indicates whether the AWS Config rule is active or is currently being deleted.
